title: Microtoena brevipedunculata
taxonomy: Microtoena brevipedunculata is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It belongs to the genus Microtoena, which consists of a small number of species found in Asia. The species is further classified into the tribe Mentheae, a group of plants that are characterized by their square stems and four-angled leaves. Microtoena brevipedunculata is native to China and is often found growing in rocky areas and mountainous regions. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies.
uses: Microtoena brevipedunculata is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Chinese and Tibetan cultures. Its medicinal uses include treating various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant's leaves and stems are used in tea form to help alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Additionally, Microtoena brevipedunculata is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its effectiveness in treating various health conditions.

intro: Microtoena brevipedunculata, commonly known as the Chinese mint or Chinese mountain mint, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of China. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as sore throats, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Microtoena brevipedunculata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical preparations, as well as being incorporated into tinctures and capsules for its medicinal benefits.
